volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 4.4.725 - Section 4.5.774b)

sections.label: Section 4.4.725
sections.original: Me deceperit , saepe obsecrans me ut veniam frustra veniet .
sections.translation: deceives me , oft as he may entreat me to come , he shall come in vain . Or else , when I ’ ve promised to come , and fixed the time , when he has carried word back for certain , and Clitipho is on the stretch of expectation , I ’ ll disappoint him and not come . Syrus will make atonement to me with his back .

sections.label: Section 4.4.729
sections.translation: She promises you very fairly .

sections.label: Section 4.4.729b
sections.translation: But do you think she is in jest ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.730
sections.translation: She ’ ll do it , if I don ’ t take care .

sections.label: Section 4.4.730b
sections.translation: They ’ re asleep [*] — I ’ faith , I ’ ll rouse them . ( Aloud .) My dear Phrygia , did you hear about the country-seat of Charinus , which that man was showing us just now ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.732b
sections.translation: I heard of it .

sections.label: Section 4.4.732c
sections.original: Proximam esse huic fundo ad dextram ?
sections.translation: That it was the next to the farm here on the right-hand side . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.4.732d
sections.translation: I remember .

sections.label: Section 4.4.733
sections.original: Curriculo percurre : apud eum miles Dionysia agitat .
sections.translation: Run thither post-haste ; the Captain is keeping the feast of Bacchus [*] at his house .

sections.label: Section 4.4.734
sections.translation: What is she going to be at ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.734b
sections.original: Dic me hic oppido esse invitam , atque asservari ;
sections.translation: Tell him I am here very much against my inclination , and am detained ;

sections.label: Section 4.4.735
sections.original: Verum aliquo pacto verba me his daturam esse , et venturam .
sections.translation: but that by some means or other I ’ ll give them the slip and come to him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.736
sections.original: Perii hercle . Bacchis , mane , mane : quo mittis istanc quaeso ?
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ m ruined ! Bacchis , stay , stay ; prithee , where are you sending her ? Order her to stop .

sections.label: Section 4.4.737b
sections.translation: Be off .

sections.label: Section 4.4.737c
sections.translation: Why , the money ’ s ready .

sections.label: Section 4.4.737d
sections.translation: Why , then I ’ ll stay .

sections.label: Section 4.4.738
sections.translation: And it will be given you presently .

sections.label: Section 4.4.738b
sections.translation: Just when you please ; do I press you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.738c
sections.translation: But do you know what you are to do , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.739
sections.translation: What ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.739b
sections.original: Transeundum nunc tibi ad Menedemum est , et tua pompa
sections.translation: You must now go over to the house of Menedemus , and your equipage

sections.label: Section 4.4.740
sections.translation: must be taken over thither .

sections.label: Section 4.4.740b
sections.translation: What scheme are you upon , you rascal ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.740c
sections.translation: What , I ? Coining money to give you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.741b
sections.translation: Do you think me a proper person for you to play upon ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.741c
sections.translation: It ’ s not without a purpose .

sections.label: Section 4.4.742
sections.original: Etiamne tecum res hic mihi est ?
sections.translation: Why , have I any business then with you here ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.742b
sections.translation: O no ; I ’ m only going to give you what ’ s your own .

sections.label: Section 4.4.743
sections.translation: Then let ’ s be going . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.4.743b
sections.translation: Follow this way . ( Goes to the door of MENEDIEMUS , and calls .) Ho there ! Dromo .

sections.label: Section 4.4.743c
sections.translation: Who is it wants me ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.743d
sections.translation: Syrus .

sections.label: Section 4.4.743e
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.744
sections.original: Ancillas omnes Bacchidis traduce huc ad vos propere .
sections.translation: Take over all the attendants of Bacchis to your house here immediately .

sections.label: Section 4.4.745
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.745b
sections.original: Ne quaeras : efferant quae secum huc attulerunt .
sections.translation: Ask no questions . Let them take what they brought here with them . The old gentleman will hope his expenses are lightened by their departure ; for sure he little knows how much loss this trifling gain will bring him . You , Dromo , if you are wise , know nothing of what you do know .

sections.label: Section 4.4.748b
sections.translation: You shall own that I ’ m dumb .

sections.label: Section 4.5.749
sections.original: Ita me Di amabunt ut nunc Menedemi vicem
sections.translation: So may the Deities prosper me , I am now concerned for the fate of Menedemus ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.750
sections.original: Miseret me tantum devenisse ad eum mali .
sections.translation: that so great a misfortune should have befallen him . To be maintaining that woman with such a retinue ! Although I am well aware he ’ ll not be sensible of it for some days to come , his son was so greatly missed by him ; but when he sees such a vast expense incurred by him

sections.label: Section 4.5.755
sections.translation: every day at home , and no limit to it , he ’ ll wish that this son would leave him a second time . See — here comes Syrus most opportunely .

sections.label: Section 4.5.757b
sections.translation: Why delay to accost him ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.757c
sections.translation: Syrus .

sections.label: Section 4.5.757d
sections.translation: Well .

sections.label: Section 4.5.758
sections.translation: How go matters ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.758b
sections.original: Te mihi ipsum iam dudum optabam dari .
sections.translation: I ’ ve been wishing for some time for you to be thrown in my way .

sections.label: Section 4.5.759
sections.original: Videre egisse iam nescio quid cum sene .
sections.translation: You seem , then , to have effected something , I know not what , with the old gentleman .

sections.label: Section 4.5.760
sections.original: De illo quod dudum ? dictum ac factum reddidi .
sections.translation: As to what we were talking of a short time since ? No sooner said than done .

sections.label: Section 4.5.761
sections.translation: In real earnest ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.761b
sections.translation: In real .

sections.label: Section 4.5.761c
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I can not forbear patting your head for it . Come here , Syrus ; I ’ ll do you some good turn for this matter , and with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 4.5.764
sections.original: At si scias , quam scite in mentem venerit .
sections.translation: But if you knew how cleverly it came into my head —

sections.label: Section 4.5.765
sections.translation: Pshaw ! Do you boast because it has turned out according to your wishes

sections.label: Section 4.5.766
sections.translation: On my word , not I , indeed ; I am telling the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.5.766b
sections.translation: Tell me how it is .

sections.label: Section 4.5.767
sections.original: Tui Clitiphonis esse amicam hanc Bacchidem
sections.translation: Clinia has told Menedemus , that this Bacchis is your Clitipho ’ s mistress , and that he has taken her thither with him in order that you might not come to know of it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.770
sections.translation: Very good .

sections.label: Section 4.5.770b
sections.translation: Tell me , please , what you think of it .

sections.label: Section 4.5.770c
sections.translation: Extremely good , I declare .

sections.label: Section 4.5.770d
sections.translation: Why yes , pretty fair . But listen , what a piece of policy still remains . He is then to say that he has seen your daughter — that her beauty charmed him as soon as he beheld her ; and that he desires her for a wife .

sections.label: Section 4.5.774b
sections.translation: What , her that has just been discovered ?
